notes: Perplexity B24v2 with thread_url_slug restored: **SIGNIFICANT PROGRESS from #299**. (1) SSE stream parsed successfully - NO MORE STREAM_FAILED_FIRST_CHUNK_ERROR. (2) thread_url_slug='apf-warn-2a844227-...' correctly extracted by frontend. (3) Page navigated to /search/apf-warn-* URL as expected. (4) BUT: frontend then does REST GET /rest/thread/apf-warn-* to load thread data from server - returns 400 because this fake thread ID doesn't exist on Perplexity's real server. (5) After failed thread fetch, page redirects back to home (perplexity.ai/?). (6) Warning text from SSE chunks was NOT rendered - the thread data fetch failure causes the page to abandon the thread view. (7) CONCLUSION: thread_url_slug fixes the first-chunk parse error, but the subsequent thread validation step is a new blocker. Need to either: (a) use a thread_url_slug format that passes validation, or (b) find a way to make the SSE chunks render before the thread fetch happens.
